# x2sys_get

```julia
x2sys_get(cmd0::String="", arg1=nothing; kwargs...)
```

Get track names from x2sys database.

(**Warning: Manual translate by Claude. Needs revision**)

Description
-----------

**x2sys_get** will return text records with the names of the track data files in the
x2sys data base for this TAG that match the given requirements. You may choose a
specific region and optionally ask only for tracks that meet certain data criteria.
Finally, you may select an option to list all possible pairs that might generate
crossovers.

Required Arguments
------------------

- **T** or **TAG** or **tag** : -- *tag="tagname"*\
   Specify the system tag for this compilation.

Optional Arguments
------------------

- **C** or **center** or **bins** : -- *bins=true*\
   Instead of reporting the track names, just output the coordinates of the center
   of each bin that has at least one track with the specified data.

- **D** or **tracknames** or **names_only** : -- *names_only=true*\
   Only report the track names [Default adds the availability of data for each field].

- **F** or **fields** or **columns** : -- *columns="name1,name2,..."*\
   Give a comma-separated list of column names (as described in the format definition
   file) that should be present [Default selects all data columns].

- **G** or **global** or **entire** : -- *entire=true*\
   Report data flags (Y or N) for the entire track rather than just for the portion
   that is inside the region set by **region** [Default].

- **L** or **pairs** or **crossover** : -- *crossover=true* **|** *crossover="listfile"* **|** *crossover=(list="file", internal=true)*\
   Crossover mode. Return a list of track pairs that should be checked for possible
   crossovers. The list is determined from the bin-index data base on the assumption
   that tracks occupying the same bin are very likely to intersect. By default we
   return all possible pairs in the data base. Append the name of a file with a list
   of tracks if you want to limit the output to those pairs that involve at least one
   of the track names in your list. The output is suitable for the **pairs** option
   in {{< gmtref x2sys_cross >}}. By default, only external crossover pairs are listed.
   Use **internal=true** to include internal pairs in the list.

- **N** or **absent** or **exclude** : -- *exclude="name1,name2,..."*\
   Give a comma-separated list of column names (as described in the format definition
   file) that must be absent.

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_R.qmd >}}

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_V.qmd >}}

Note
----

The tracks that are returned all have the requested data (**columns**) within the
specified region (**region**). Furthermore, the columns of Y and N for other data
types also reflect the content of the track portion within the selected region,
unless **entire** is set.

Examples
--------

To find all tracks associated with the tag MGD77, restricted to a certain region
in the south Pacific, and have at least free air anomalies and bathymetry:

```julia
using GMT
D = x2sys_get(tag="MGD77", region=(180,240,-60,-30), columns="faa,depth")
```

To find all tracks associated with the tag MGD77 that have depth but not twt:

```julia
using GMT
D = x2sys_get(tag="MGD77", columns="depth", exclude="twt")
```

To find all pairs associated with the tag MGD77 that might intersect each other,
but only those pairs which involve tracks in your list new.lis:

```julia
using GMT
D = x2sys_get(tag="MGD77", crossover="new.lis")
```

To include internal crossover pairs in the list:

```julia
using GMT
D = x2sys_get(tag="MGD77", crossover=(list="new.lis", internal=true))
```

To get bin centers instead of track names:

```julia
using GMT
D = x2sys_get(tag="MGD77", bins=true, region=(180,240,-60,-30))
```

See Also
--------

- {{< gmtref x2sys_init >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_cross >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_binlist >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_datalist >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_list >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_put >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_solve >}}
- [Alphabetical Function List](../alphabetical.html)

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
using GMT
GMT.doc_source_links("x2sys_get")
```
